name: emonk
documentation:
# emonk

A journal app written in [Python]. Uses the [cryptography] package for security.

## How to install it

	pip3 install emonk

## How to use it

	emonk <words or tags to search for>

### Command line options

    -h --help               Show help.
    -e --edit               Edit entries.
    --from-date FROM_DATE   Get entries from this date.
    -n N                    Get last N entries.
    -t --tags               List tags.
    --to-date TO_DATE       Get entries to this date.
    -v --version            Show version number.

### Write a journal entry

    emonk

### Logical AND search

    emonk apples oranges

### Logical OR search
Enclose the OR with quotes.

    emonk "apples|oranges"

### Edit entries containing "apples"

    emonk -e apples

### Import journal entries from text file

    emonk < lastweek.txt

### Export journal entries to text file

    emonk > exported.txt

## Custom datetime format
Emonk stores journal entries using the ISO 8601 datetime format: yyyy-mm-dd hh-mm. To use another [datetime format] add it to the .emonk config file in your home directory:

    date_format: "%m/%d/%y %I:%M %p"

## Links

- [Project page](https://seagrape.us/emonk.html)
- [Changelog](https://seagrape.us/emonk-history.html)

## Contact me

Send bug reports and patches to <craig@seagrape.us>.

[Python]: https://www.python.org
[cryptography]: https://github.com/pyca/cryptography
[datetime format]: https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#format-codes

functions.name: emonk.io.join
documentation:
Join datestamp: entry dict into string.

Args:
    d (dict): dictionary of journal entries.
    custom_format (bool): use custom date format.

Returns:
    journal entries as a string.

functions.name: emonk.io.read_stdin
documentation:
If stdin has text, import it and exit.

Example:
    emonk < file.txt

functions.name: emonk.io.split
documentation:
Split string s into dictionary.

Args:
    s (str): journal entries.
    custom_format (bool): use custom date format.

Returns:
    dict: datestamp:entry string.

functions.name: emonk.io.read
documentation:
Read journal file and convert to dictionary.

Note:
    The journal file datestamps and dictionary keys use ISO format. This
    allows for easy sorting by date, and easy conversion to a custom
    datetime format.

Returns:
    dict: datestamp:entry string.

functions.name: emonk.io.write
documentation: Convert dictionary to string and write to journal file.

functions.name: emonk.io.iso2custom
documentation: Convert datetime string s from ISO to custom date format.

functions.name: emonk.io.custom2iso
documentation: Convert datetime string s from custom to ISO date format.

modules.name: emonk.crypto
documentation: Crypto module.

functions.name: emonk.crypto.genkey
documentation:
Generate crypto key.

Args:
    pw (str): password.
    salt (bytes): encryption salt. Default is None.

Note:
    Code pulled directly from cryptography module docs.

functions.name: emonk.crypto.encrypt
documentation: Return string s encrypted with salt prepended.

modules.name: emonk.__main__
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python3

functions.name: emonk.config.read
documentation:
Read config file if found, or run setup().

Returns:
    config dictionary.

functions.name: emonk.config.setup
documentation:
Setup config file.

Returns:
    config dictionary.

functions.name: emonk.journal.contains
documentation:
Return True if string s contains all terms.

Args:
    s (str): journal entry body.
    terms (list): keywords or tags.

Note:
    tags require whole word search: #blue-mountain and #blue are two
    different tags with different meanings. But if you're searching
    for the keyword blue, returning blue or blue-mountain is useful.

functions.name: emonk.journal.diff
documentation:
Compare two dictionaries.

Args:
    d1 (dict)
    d2 (dict)

Returns:
    string with totals for add, modify, delete.

functions.name: emonk.journal.edit
documentation:
Edit selected entries.

Args:
    args (Namespace): ArgumentParser Namespace.

Returns:
    string with add, delete, modify count.

functions.name: emonk.journal.edit_file
documentation:
Edit file.

Args:
    s (str): text file input string. Default: ''.

Returns:
    Output string.

functions.name: emonk.journal.get_last_n
documentation:
Get last (n) entries.

Args:
    d (dict): dictionary of journal entries.
    args (Namespace): ArgumentParser Namespace.

Returns:
    dict of journal entries.

functions.name: emonk.journal.search_date_range
documentation:
Find journal entries within date range.

Args:
    d (dict): dictionary of journal entries.
    args (Namespace): ArgumentParser Namespace.

Returns:
    dict of journal entries within date range.

functions.name: emonk.journal.print_contains
documentation:
Print entries w/ keywords.

Args:
    args (Namespace): ArgumentParser Namespace.

functions.name: emonk.journal.print_last_n
documentation: Print last (n) entries.

functions.name: emonk.journal.print_tag_table
documentation: Print list of tag:count.

functions.name: emonk.journal.find
documentation:
Find journal entries containing keywords or tags.

Args:
    d (dict): dictionary of journal entries.
    terms (list): keywords or tags to search for.

Returns:
    dictionary of journal entries.

functions.name: emonk.journal.get_tag_count
documentation:
Return dict of tag:count found in journal.

Args:
    d (dict): dictionary of journal entries.

Returns:
    dictionary: tag:count.

functions.name: emonk.journal.compose
documentation:
Compose a single journal entry.

Returns:
    string with add count.

functions.name: emonk.journal.validate
documentation: Raise FormatError if s isn't a valid date or datetime.
